# Transcrição: 19. Trait 18 - Great Salespeople hold themselves to performance standards higher than normal.

- **Fonte:** 19. Trait 18 - Great Salespeople hold themselves to performance standards higher than normal.
- **Duração:** 01:01
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:01]

Great salespeople hold themselves to performance standards higher than normal and definitely higher than expected from others. The great salespeople do not compare themselves to others actions, other statistics. They don't compare themselves to the results of average people. The greats push themselves, push themselves internally to performance beyond what the marketplace is attained. Look, they're pushing themselves to another place. Think Roger Bannister. Think the greats in any profession. The greats listen to the industry stats. They listen and look at metrics of the industry and then they laugh at them quietly maybe knowing they refuse to compare themselves to those results, statistics and metrics that were produced by those that have only accepted being average. Look, be great. Look at the norms and the stats and the metrics and then see what you can do to be greater than that.
